info.name: LintPDF API (Tenant)
info.description: Tenant-facing LintPDF routes only. Generated from https://api.lintpdf.com/openapi.tenant.json. Set {{API_KEY}} to your ``lpdf_live_...`` key before sending requests.

request.description:
List all annotations for a job (tenant-scoped).

Pass ``?include=comments`` to embed each annotation's full comment
thread inline, eliminating the N+1 fan-out that previously required
a ``GET .../annotations/{id}/comments`` round trip per annotation.
Default shape is unchanged for back-compat.

Create an annotation on a job page (authenticated dashboard writer).

Attribution precedence:
  1. ``X-Visitor-Email`` header (forwarded by the Next.js plugin,
     carries the authenticated user's email so per-user audit works
     on tenants with multiple dashboard reviewers).
  2. ``tenant.contact_email`` — fallback for direct API-key callers.
  3. ``"dashboard@lintpdf"`` sentinel so the column is never NULL.

List annotations for a share-link job (unauthenticated read).

Pass ``?include=comments`` to embed each annotation's comment thread
inline. The share-link surface is always read-only for comments; the
``allow_annotations`` flag on the token only gates *creation* of new
annotations, not retrieval of the thread on existing ones.

Create an annotation via a share-link token.

Requires the token's ``allow_annotations`` flag to be set (minted by
the issuing tenant) and an ``X-Visitor-Email`` header identifying
the writer. The email is recorded once per token+email pair in
``share_link_visitors`` for audit.
Get the tenant's AI configuration.
Update tenant AI configuration (self-service fields only).
Set custom spell-check dictionary words.
Upload a reference logo for logo verification.
Remove a reference logo.
Set the brand color palette for color compliance checks.
View current AI credit balance and package status.

Return every AI-credit package for the authenticated tenant.

Drives the "Package history" table on the tenant credits page.
Doesn't require check_ai_access — customers should see their own
history even if ai is disabled.

Create a Stripe Checkout session for a credit top-up purchase.

The endpoint does NOT insert a package row. The package is only
inserted when Stripe posts back ``checkout.session.completed`` to
our webhook, which calls
:py:func:`lintpdf.billing.allocation.fulfill_purchase`. This is
the standard Stripe pattern — the server never trusts that the
customer actually paid until Stripe says so.

Generate a Preflight Profile from a natural language description.

Uses an LLM to interpret the description and produce a valid
Preflight Profile JSON configuration.
Plain-language AI review of a job's preflight findings.
List available AI presets.
Get details of a specific AI preset.
View AI usage report with filtering.
Get AI usage trend data for SPC dashboard.
Read the tenant's per-month LLM cost-cap configuration + usage.
Update cost-cap configuration. Tenant-scope only.
Public info endpoint for the approver landing page.
Return the chain attached to the job (if any) identified by report token.

Submit a batch of PDFs for preflight processing.

Accepts multipart form data with one or more file fields and an
optional ``profile_id`` (defaults to ``lintpdf-default``). Each file
becomes an individual Job; they are linked by a synthetic
``batch_id`` stored in Redis with a 24h TTL.

File fields are accepted in any of these shapes:
    files=...&files=...          (repeated, traditional HTML)
    files[]=...&files[]=...      (PHP-style array)
    files[0]=...&files[1]=...    (indexed array, what Playwright sends)
    file_0=...&file_1=...        (numbered singular)
Get the status of a batch operation.
Get aggregated summary of all completed jobs in a batch.

Receive + verify Stripe webhook events.

Unlike the other LintPDF endpoints this one accepts no API key —
it's public and gated entirely by the Stripe-Signature header,
which the engine verifies with the shared webhook secret. Any
mismatch or missing header returns 400.
Return the tenant's default-output-branding mode.

Update the tenant's default output branding.

Modes:
* ``anonymous`` — strip all branding + sanitise PDF metadata by default
  (broker → distributor use case). Sets ``unbranded_by_default = True``
  and clears ``default_brand_profile_id``.
* ``profile`` — use a specific BrandProfile by default. Requires a
  valid ``brand_profile_id`` owned by the tenant.
* ``lintpdf`` — fall back to LintPDF's built-in branding (clears both).
Return the current state of the tenant's white-label app/viewer domain.
Customer self-service: set (or clear) the tenant's app/viewer custom domain.
List all brand profiles for the tenant.
Create a new brand profile.
Get a brand profile by ID.
Update a brand profile.
Delete a brand profile.

Customer self-service: set a per-brand-profile custom report domain.

Useful for agencies serving multiple clients — each brand profile
can point reports at the owning client's own subdomain.
Upload a logo for a brand profile.
Return the current state of the tenant's white-label custom report domain.

Customer self-service: set (or clear) the tenant's white-label custom domain.

Setting always resets ``brand_custom_domain_verified`` to False —
only the admin flow (or the DNS probe task) can flip it back to True.

Returns 403 if the tenant's plan doesn't include the whitelabel
entitlement (SCALE/ENTERPRISE only), 409 if another tenant already
claims this domain, 422 if the domain is on the blocklist or
otherwise invalid.
Set or clear the tenant's default brand profile.
Get tenant color management configuration.
Update color management configuration.
List available gamut conditions.
Set brand color palette for color compliance checking.
Get current Pantone color overrides for a tenant.

Bulk set / replace all Pantone color overrides for a tenant.

Names are normalized to uppercase with collapsed whitespace so they
match ``PantoneManager`` lookup keys.
Clear all Pantone overrides for a tenant.
Upload a custom ICC profile for gamut checking.
Remove a custom ICC profile.
List all custom endpoints for the current tenant.
Create a custom API endpoint bound to a profile.
Update a custom endpoint.
Delete a custom endpoint.

Submit a PDF to a custom endpoint, using its bound profile.

``identifier`` may be either the endpoint slug (normal case) or its
UUID (backwards compat for clients that only track the ID).
Every file pack for the authenticated tenant (history table).
Current file-quota balance + this month's allotment.
Create a Stripe Checkout session for a file-pack purchase.
Detailed service status including database, Redis, and queue info.
Basic health check — always returns ok if the service is running.
List every import mapping owned by the current tenant.

Create a new import mapping.

The mapping config is validated by instantiating
:class:`CustomMappingParser`; a ``ParserError`` becomes a 422.

Soft-delete by flipping ``is_active`` to false.

Keeps historical rows around so a job that was submitted with this
mapping still has a config to point back to for audit.

Run the mapping against a sample payload without persisting anything.

Lets tenants iterate on a config in the editor: the request may
override ``config`` and ``sample_payload``; anything not overridden
falls back to the stored mapping row. Parser errors surface as
``ok=false`` with a human-readable ``error`` instead of an HTTP 422
so the UI can render inline feedback.
List jobs for the current tenant (paginated).
Get job status and results.
Cancel or delete a job.

Return the universal state of a preflight job in one call.

Stitches together the five retrieval surfaces callers currently have
to fan out across:

1. Core job (`GET /jobs/{id}`) — status, preflight summary + findings,
   auto-attached report URLs.
2. Reports (`GET /jobs/{id}/reports`) — every minted token with
   share-link metadata (`allow_annotations`, `require_visitor_email`).
3. Approval chain (`GET /jobs/{id}/approval-chain`) — status +
   step history including each approver's free-text `notes`.
4. Verdict (`GET /viewer/jobs/{id}/verdict`) — manual pass/fail
   marker + aggregated notes + the auto-passed flag mirrored from
   the preflight summary.
5. Annotations (`GET /viewer/jobs/{id}/annotations`) with threaded
   comments embedded inline (no N+1; comments are joined in a single
   query via ``annotation_id IN (...)``).

Use ``?include=approval_chain,verdict`` to trim the payload to just
the sections you need. Unknown include keys return 422.
List all available preflight profiles (builtins + tenant's custom).
Create a custom preflight profile.
Get detailed profile configuration.
Delete a custom profile. Built-in profiles cannot be deleted.

Return the human-friendly check name registry.

Static data — clients should cache aggressively.
Generate hosted reports for a completed job.
List existing report tokens for a job.
Revoke a report token and delete from storage.

Validate a report token and return job metadata.

Used by the Fairy Ring plugin to verify public viewer access tokens
before proxying viewer API requests to the engine.

Get findings for a job via report token (public, no auth).

Returns the same finding data as GET /api/v1/jobs/{job_id} but
authenticated by report token instead of tenant API key.
Serve an interactive HTML report by token (public, no auth).

Serve a JSON report by token (public, no auth).

Same shape as the LintPDF v1 import schema — re-importable via
``preflight_source=external``, ``external_format=lintpdf_json``.

Serve a PDF-flavoured report by token (public, no auth).

Handles all three PDF-bearing token formats — plain ``pdf``, the
findings-overlay ``annotated_pdf``, and the reviewer-markup
``annotated_pdf_markup`` — since every one of them lives behind a
``{report_base}/r/{token}.pdf`` URL. The storage key encodes the
specific format (see ``InMemoryStorage.upload_report`` /
``S3Storage.upload_report`` — ``reports/{tenant}/{job}/report.{fmt}``),
so we must pass ``record.format`` through rather than hard-coding
``"pdf"``.

Serve an XML report by token (public, no auth).

Same field taxonomy as the JSON report — for Switch / MIS / other
XML-only consumers.
Get current daily rate limit usage for the authenticated tenant.
Update user AI access configuration.
Start an AI trial for a user (30 days).
Compare two jobs' PDFs and return per-page similarity scores.
Serve a cached comparison diff image (red highlights where pixels differ).

Queue a one-off analyzer run to populate a missing viewer capability.

Only applies to jobs whose ``data_capabilities[capability]`` is false —
typically minimal or external-import jobs where the imported report
didn't cover this tool.

query.description: Branding override: 'anonymous' (strip all branding), 'lintpdf' (LintPDF default), or a BrandProfile UUID.

request.description: Return resolved viewer configuration for this job's tenant + brand profile.
List PDF Optional Content Groups (layers).
Return page count and dimensions for all pages in a job's PDF.
Render a single ink channel as a grayscale PNG.

Sample per-channel ink coverage and TAC at a point on a PDF page.

Runs Ghostscript ``tiffsep`` to decompose the page into CMYK + spot
channel rasters, then reads a 3x3 patch around the sampled pixel on
each channel. Returns ``{channels: [{name, percent}], tac,
limit_exceeded}``. Every plan tier has access — this is a free QA
tool, not a gated premium capability.
Return dimensions and box info for a single page.

query.description: Render DPI at which the point was sampled. Higher values resolve finer geometry. 72-600, defaults to 300.

request.description:
Sample the color at a point on a PDF page.

Renders the page at the requested DPI and reads the pixel at the
given PDF coordinate (origin lower-left, in points).

query.description: Total area coverage threshold as a percentage (100-500). Pixels at or above this value are highlighted in the heatmap. Defaults to 300% (typical for sheetfed offset).

request.description: Render a TAC heatmap overlay as an RGBA PNG.

query.description: Total area coverage threshold in percent (100-500).

request.description:
Return per-text-run mean TAC metadata for tooltip overlays.

Coordinates are in PDF points with origin at the **top-left** of the
page (matching poppler's ``pdftotext -bbox`` output). ``exceeds`` is
pre-computed against ``tac_limit`` so the frontend can style the
tooltip without re-comparing.

query.description: OCG (layer) indices to force **visible**, overriding the PDF's /D/OFF defaults. Indices match ``ocg_index`` from ``GET /api/v1/viewer/jobs/{id}/layers``. Accepts a repeated query param (``ocg_on=0&ocg_on=3``) or a comma-list (``ocg_on=0,3``).
OCG (layer) indices to force **hidden**. Same index convention as ``ocg_on``. An index that appears in both ``ocg_on`` and ``ocg_off`` is rejected with 422.

request.description:
Render a single page as a PNG tile at the requested DPI.

Tile read path (fastest first):
1. Redis hot byte-cache (opt-in, ~1-3 ms).
2. S3 tile cache (pre-warmed by ``warm_viewer_tiles`` after
   preflight completion - this is where the bytes live durably).
3. Render on demand via ``render_page_to_image`` and populate
   both caches for the next reader.

When ``ocg_on`` / ``ocg_off`` are omitted the response is
byte-identical to the legacy default-state tile and shares its
cache key, so S3 tiles warmed by ``warm_viewer_tiles`` still hit.
List all ink channels (CMYK + spot colors) in the job's PDF.

Report the current tile-warming progress for a job.

Reads from the Redis hash written by the
``lintpdf.viewer.warm_tiles`` Celery task. The viewer polls this
every 1.5 s until ``status`` settles into ``complete``, ``failed``,
or ``disabled``.
Get the current verdict for a job.
Set a manual verdict (pass/fail) on a job.

Public: get viewer configuration for a share-link viewer.

Branding is read from the :class:`ReportToken` snapshot captured at
mint time — so the shared viewer keeps the brand the broker chose
regardless of later tenant setting changes.
Public: list PDF layers (OCGs).
Public: list pages via report token.
Public: render a separation channel as grayscale PNG.
Public: per-channel CMYK + spot ink densitometer reading.
Public: get page dimensions and box info.

query.description: Render DPI at which the point was sampled. Higher values resolve finer geometry. 72-600, defaults to 300.

request.description: Public: densitometer color sample.

query.description: Total area coverage threshold as a percentage (100-500). Pixels at or above this value are highlighted in the heatmap.

request.description: Public: render TAC heatmap overlay as RGBA PNG.
Public: per-text-run mean TAC metadata for tooltip overlays.
Public: render a page tile as PNG.
Public: list ink separation channels.

Public: email the interactive viewer link to one or more recipients.

Anyone with the token can forward the report. Rate-limited implicitly
by the viewer page's email gate.

Public share-link mirror of ``/api/v1/jobs/{id}/state``.

Returns the same shape the tenant-authenticated digest does **minus**
the ``reports`` section — listing every share-link token for the job
from a single token would leak sibling shares that the current
visitor wasn't handed. Everything else (preflight summary, verdict
with notes, approval chain with per-step notes, annotations with
comments embedded) is already exposed via the existing public
endpoints; this endpoint just stitches them into one round trip.

Accepted ``?include=`` keys: ``approval_chain``, ``verdict``,
``annotations`` (default = all three).
Public: tile-warming progress for a share-link viewer.
Public: get verdict (read-only).
List all registered webhook endpoints for the current tenant.
Register a new webhook endpoint.

List webhook delivery attempts for the authenticated tenant.

Newest first. Filter by ``webhook_id``, ``event``, or
``success=false`` to narrow in on failures. Paginates via
``?page=`` + ``?page_size=`` (default 50, capped at 200).
Fetch one delivery row + the exact signed payload we POSTed.

Re-fire a previously-recorded delivery against its original URL.

Creates a NEW ``WebhookDelivery`` row with the same event + payload
so the audit log grows rather than mutating history. The endpoint's
current secret signs the replayed body (if it was rotated since
the original attempt, the replay will sign with the new secret --
callers who rotate secrets lose replayability of older bodies,
which is the expected security tradeoff).
Update a webhook endpoint (URL, events, or active status).
Remove a webhook endpoint.
Send a test payload to a webhook endpoint to verify connectivity.
Rotate the per-endpoint signing secret. Returns the new secret once; subsequent reads return the masked tail. Old secret stays valid for a brief grace period to allow consumer rollover.
Generate (or fetch cached) AI-Explain text for a finding via Claude Haiku 4.5 (Q-C4/C5). Returns 402 when the tenant's monthly cost cap is exhausted.
EPM candidacy verdict for a job (tier, rejection_drivers, advisories, recommends_indichrome, legacy_codes_fired, epm_findings_count).
List decisions on a job (newest first; active by default).
Record a job-level decision (no finding scope).
Record a finding-level decision.
Soft-revoke a decision (Q-2). Idempotent: re-revoking a revoked decision is a no-op.
List workflows for the current tenant.
Create a workflow pinning a profile + brand spec.
Update a workflow's editable fields.
Delete a workflow.
